title: Meta's AI Tool Promises Quick Cash for Content Creators
summary: Meta's acquired AI company, Manus, is running ads offering easy money by using AI to build websites for local businesses. The campaign pays creators to pitch these services to businesses. This raises questions about the sustainability and ethics of such get-rich-quick schemes.
body:
Meta's recently acquired AI company, Manus, is running ads that promise quick and easy money. The scheme involves finding local businesses without websites or with poorly designed ones, using AI to build them a new website, and then selling the service to the business. Manus is paying content creators to promote and execute this plan as part of their campaign.

This approach raises questions about the sustainability and ethics of such get-rich-quick schemes. While the idea might appeal to those looking for a side hustle, it could lead to low-quality websites and frustrated business owners. It also highlights the growing trend of AI being used to automate and scale traditional business services, often with mixed results.

If you're considering trying this out, be cautious. While the promise of easy money is enticing, the reality might be more complex. Focus on providing genuine value to businesses rather than just selling a quick fix. Keep an eye out for similar schemes and always research thoroughly before jumping into any get-rich-quick opportunity.
twitterThread:
🚨 Meta's AI tool is promising quick cash. Here's what you need to know:

Meta's AI company Manus is running ads offering easy money by using AI to build websites for local businesses. Content creators are being paid to pitch these services. #AI #Meta

In plain English: Find a business with a bad website, use AI to build a better one, then sell it to them. Manus is paying creators to do this. Sounds too good to be true? Maybe.

The part that matters: This is a get-rich-quick scheme. Be cautious.
If you've ever wanted to make money with AI, this might seem like an easy way out. But think about it: Are these websites actually good? Will businesses be happy with the results? Probably not.
Here's what this means for you: While the promise of easy money is tempting, the reality might be more complex. Focus on providing real value, not just quick fixes.
If you're considering trying this out, do your research. Look for genuine opportunities to help businesses, not just sell them a quick AI-generated website.
standaloneTweet: Meta's AI tool is promising quick cash by having creators build and sell websites to local businesses. Sounds too good to be true? Be cautious. #AI #Meta
